extra.message: User-controlled request.path is interpolated via f-string into render_template_string(), allowing Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). An attacker can craft a URL path containing Jinja2 template syntax (e.g. {{config}}) to leak application secrets or achieve RCE.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Flask secret key 'super secret key' is hardcoded in application source. This key is used to sign session cookies; anyone who knows it can forge valid session cookies.

Hardcoded static registration code 'a36e990b-0024-4d55-b74a-f8d7528e1764' is seeded into the database. This registration code is publicly known and allows bypass of the invite-only registration mechanism.
Default admin user account ('admin@evfa.com' with password 'admin') is created in the seed script. The password is hardcoded and the account always exists in seeded databases.

User-controlled 'preferences' cookie is base64-decoded then passed to pickle.loads() with no integrity verification. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload to achieve arbitrary remote code execution on the server.

User input from request.args.get('search') is concatenated directly into a raw SQL string via f-string in sqlalchemy.text(), bypassing parameterized queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g. ' UNION SELECT ...) to read, modify, or destroy database records.

The /accounts/<int:user_id>/notes endpoint checks @login_required but does not verify that user_id matches current_user.id. Any authenticated user can access the private notes of any other user (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The POST /account route (update_account) is missing the @login_required decorator, allowing unauthenticated users to submit account update requests. Combined with the mass-assignment vulnerability below, this is critical.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: User-supplied form data (only excluding 'password') is used to directly update current_user.__dict__ via mass assignment. The AccountForm includes an 'is_admin' BooleanField, allowing any user to set is_admin=True and escalate privileges to administrator.

cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

extra.message: User-provided registration code is concatenated directly into a raw SQL string via f-string in sqlalchemy.text(). An attacker can craft a malicious registration code to perform SQL injection and bypass the registration token check.

User-supplied URL from the image form is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() with no validation. An attacker can specify file:// URLs (e.g. file:///etc/passwd) to read arbitrary local files, or internal URLs for SSRF attacks against internal services.

The /notes/<int:note_id>/delete endpoint checks @login_required but does not verify that the note belongs to current_user. Any authenticated user can delete any note in the database by guessing or enumerating note IDs (IDOR).

Note text is rendered with the 'safe' Jinja2 filter ({{ note.text | safe }}), which disables HTML escaping. Since note text is user-supplied via the CKEditor form and stored without sanitization, this is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: render_table() is called with safe_columns=['text'], which tells bootstrap-flask to render the 'text' column as raw HTML without escaping. Since note text is user-supplied and unsanitized, this is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: render_table() is called with safe_columns=['text'], which tells bootstrap-flask to render the 'text' column as raw HTML without escaping. Since note text is user-supplied and unsanitized, this is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
